3A_Bibliographic.citation: Raney, J. H., Al-shoaibi, A. A., Ganson, K. T., Testa, A., Jackson, D. B., Singh, G., Sajjad, O. M., & Nagata, J. M. (2023). Associations between adverse childhood experiences and early adolescent problematic screen use in the United States. BMC Public Health, 23, 1213.

3B_Sample_Data.verbatim_sample_description: Prospective data from the Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development Study (Baseline and Year 2; 2018-2020; N = 9,673, participants who did not use screens were excluded) were analyzed in 2023.
3B_Sample_Data.notes: Exposure (ACEs) at baseline; outcomes (problematic screen use) at Year 2.

3C_Exposure.exposure_variable: Adverse Childhood Experiences (ACE) score (0, 1, 2, 3, 4+) at baseline
3C_Exposure.exposure_instrument: ABCD ACE items based on CDC-Kaiser ACE study (9 of 10 ACEs assessed; emotional abuse not included)
3C_Exposure.modalities: N/A - ACEs are the exposure; screen use outcomes are the dependent variables
3C_Exposure.verbatim_exposure_description: ACE score was calculated through adolescent and parent responses from the baseline (2016-2018) survey. The ABCD study assesses nine of ten ACEs reflecting the items in the original CDC-Kaiser ACE study across different surveys as a validated ACEs screener was not administered.
3C_Exposure.notes: NOTE: In this paper, screen time is the OUTCOME, not the exposure. ACEs are the exposure. This is relevant because the screen-time variables are dependent variables.

3D_Outcome.primary_outcome: Problematic screen use (video game, social media, mobile phone) - binary cutoffs
3D_Outcome.outcome_instrument: Video Game Addiction Questionnaire (VGAQ), Social Media Addiction Questionnaire (SMAQ), Mobile Phone Involvement Questionnaire (MPIQ)
3D_Outcome.base_rate_if_binary: 7.0% problematic video game use, 3.5% problematic social media use, 21.8% problematic mobile phone use
3D_Outcome.verbatim_outcome_description: The Video Game Addiction Questionnaire (VGAQ) is a six-question instrument. A cutoff score of 4 or greater was characterized as problematic video game use. The Social Media Addiction Questionnaire (SMAQ) is a six-question survey. A cutoff score of 4 or greater was characterized as problematic social media use. The Mobile Phone Involvement Questionnaire (MPIQ) is an eight-item survey. Based on prior literature, scores of four or greater were considered problematic mobile phone use.

3E_Design_Analysis.model_type_detail: Generalized logistic mixed effects models with multiple imputation
3E_Design_Analysis.covariates_listed: age, sex, race/ethnicity, highest parent education, household income, adolescent anxiety, depression, attention-deficit symptoms, study site, twins
3E_Design_Analysis.verbatim_analysis_description: Generalized logistic mixed effects models estimated prospective associations between baseline ACE score and problematic video game, social media, and mobile phone use (binary outcomes) among youth who reported that type of screen use. Both analyses were adjusted for potential confounders listed above, accounting for study site and twins. We performed multiple imputation by chained equations using the R package 'mice'.

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Adjusted OR from logistic mixed effects model
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: ACEs >= 4+: OR 3.11*** (2.05-4.73) for problematic video game use (adjusted model)

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Adjusted OR from logistic mixed effects model
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: ACEs >= 4+: OR 1.52 (0.83-2.79) for problematic social media use (adjusted model)

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Adjusted OR from logistic mixed effects model
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: ACEs >= 4+: OR 1.57*** (1.22-2.03) for problematic mobile phone use (adjusted model)

3F_Effect_Sizes.outcome_scale_note: Adjusted OR from logistic mixed effects model
3F_Effect_Sizes.verbatim_quote: ACEs = 1: OR 1.68** (1.17-2.41) for problematic video game use (adjusted model)

3G_Reporting_Quality.abstract_significance_verbatim: ACEs were associated with higher problematic video game and mobile phone use in both unadjusted and adjusted models
3G_Reporting_Quality.large_n_verbatim: This large, demographically diverse, national sample
3G_Reporting_Quality.notes: Uses asterisk notation (*p<0.05, **p<0.01, ***p<0.001). ORs for the video game outcome are genuinely large (3.11 for 4+ ACEs), so small-ES acknowledgment is less applicable for that outcome.

3H_Recommendations.recommendation_verbatim: Clinicians should be aware of the increased risk of problematic screen use among youth with high ACE scores, explore video game, social media, and mobile phone use among this population, and collaborate with families to implement a family media use plan. Schools may consider implementing curricula focused on promoting healthy digital habits.

3I_Rhetorical_Moves.discussion_characterization: The discussion emphasizes the dose-dependent relationship between ACEs and problematic screen use, particularly for video games and mobile phones. It contextualizes findings within prior literature on ACEs and addictive behaviors. The paper notes the mixed results for social media (significant in linear but not logistic models, possibly due to low base rate of 3.5% at this age). Recommendations are directed at clinicians and schools to address problematic screen use in trauma-exposed populations.
3I_Rhetorical_Moves.ambiguity_notes: Note that in this paper, screen use is the OUTCOME predicted by ACEs, not the typical exposure-to-outcome direction for screen time research.
